site.title: Niuniu — Local-first parallel AI work platform
site.description: A local-first AI work platform: write down a goal and many agents finish it in parallel on Claude Code / Codex / any model — from software to office docs, data analysis, and content.

hero.headline: Set the goal. An agent team finishes it.
hero.subhead: Niuniu is a local-first AI work platform: write the work as issues, and many agents plan, execute, and verify in parallel on Claude Code / Codex / any model — from writing code to building spreadsheets, querying data, and drafting docs. It only comes back when truly blocked.
hero.dashboard_alt: Screenshot of the Niuniu workstation: parallel task list, chat panel, commit feed, and terminal

goal_loop.section_subtitle: You define the goal; the agent owns plan → execute → verify on its own, and only comes back to you when it actually needs you.

steps.title: Write the goal
steps.desc: File the work as an issue — one sentence is enough; no need to spell out the code.

steps.title: Agent plans
steps.desc: Inside the workspace, the agent reads context, breaks the work down, and proposes a plan.

steps.title: Parallel execution
steps.desc: Many workspaces pursue their own goals in parallel; each agent works directly in its own git worktree.

steps.title: Self-verification
steps.desc: After changes, the agent runs tests, reads the diff, self-reviews, and iterates on failures.

steps.title: Only when blocked
steps.desc: You only get pinged for real blockers — missing credentials, decisions, risky actions. Otherwise it ships quietly.

deploy.section_subtitle: Two flavors — from solo desktop to enterprise self-host.

features.section_title: One platform across software · office · data · content

items.title: Goal-driven, autohosted loop
items.desc: Write the goal; the agent plans, executes, verifies, and wraps up on its own — an unattended autohost watchdog keeps it going and only pings you when blocked.

items.title: Parallel workspaces, worktree-isolated
items.desc: Each workspace gets its own git worktree + isolated agent session, running in parallel across projects and repos without collisions.

items.title: Any agent, any model — no lock-in
items.desc: Drive Claude Code / Codex / Qwen Code, and point them at GLM / DeepSeek / MiniMax / Kimi or any compatible endpoint via scene env presets.

items.title: Kanban + Harness engineering gates
items.desc: Track issues / checklists / comments / timeline; Harness wires lint / tests / AI review into a pre-commit gate.

items.title: Scenes: one-click work modes
items.desc: 18 built-in scenes spanning software / office / data / content, declaratively projecting MCP + plugins + quick actions — auto-recommended, layerable, forkable.

items.title: Data intelligence: query + live dashboards
items.desc: Connect SQL / Redis / Mongo / ES / HTTP, run governed read queries, and pin inline charts as continuously refreshing dashboards.

items.title: Knowledge base + project memory
items.desc: Ingest local directories into a searchable knowledge base; a white-box project memory captures patterns, decisions, and gotchas — searchable, mergeable, restorable.

items.title: IM bots: Lark / DingTalk / Telegram
items.desc: Drive work conversationally from group chat — inbound text & images, markdown out, one bot routed across multiple projects.

screenshot.caption: One workstation. Many parallel AI Agent sessions.

demo.section_subtitle: Turn single-session Claude Code's “one chat you drive” into many workspaces driving many agents at once — each picking its own flow from your kanban stages, looping autonomously.
demo.alt: Niuniu demo animation: 3 workspaces each running an agent, each picking its own kanban stages, looping autonomously in parallel
demo.caption: 3 workspaces, each driving one issue; agents advance in parallel, fully isolated, finishing on their own.
demo.note: Illustrative animation (not a screen capture of the desktop app), shown to convey the parallel scheduling model.

comparison.section_title: Niuniu vs Claude Desktop vs OpenAI Codex
comparison.section_subtitle: All three run agents in parallel and support MCP. The difference: Niuniu isn't locked to one vendor, spans software through office / data / content, and can run entirely on your own machine or network.
comparison.footnote: Compiled from publicly available 2026 product information; competitor capabilities evolve — for reference only.

rows.label: Model / engine
rows.niuniu: Drives Claude Code / Codex / Qwen + any compatible endpoint (GLM / DeepSeek / Kimi), switchable per workspace — no vendor lock-in

rows.label: Work types
rows.niuniu: Software + office + data + content, 18 built-in scenes one click away

rows.label: How work is organized
rows.niuniu: Kanban, issue-driven: columns / checklists / comments / timeline / execution plans

rows.label: Parallel execution
rows.niuniu: Many workspaces, each its own worktree, scheduled across projects from one board
rows.claude: Cowork can run parallel subagents (within a local folder)
rows.codex: Built-in worktrees, multiple agents in parallel / cloud

rows.label: Workflow & gates
rows.niuniu: Harness engineering gates (pre-commit / AI-review) + kanban stages + unattended autohost watchdog

rows.label: Data intelligence
rows.niuniu: Connect SQL / Redis / Mongo / ES / HTTP, governed queries + pinned live dashboards

rows.label: IM collaboration
rows.niuniu: Lark / DingTalk / Telegram two-way conversational, routed across projects

rows.label: Knowledge & memory
rows.niuniu: Knowledge-base ingestion + white-box searchable project memory

rows.label: Deployment & data sovereignty
rows.niuniu: Local-first (SQLite); self-hosted team edition on docker + PG, multi-tenant / audit, data can stay on-prem
rows.claude: Cloud account (Cowork accesses a local folder)

rows.label: MCP support
rows.niuniu: Yes — and exposes its own context to agents as tools

social_proof.section_title: Developers are shipping in parallel with Niuniu
social_proof.section_subtitle: Free to use, local-first, actively iterating — follow releases and feedback on GitHub.

stats.label: Local-first · data stays on your machine by default

testimonials.quote: Single-session Claude Code only let me watch one task at a time. With three workspaces running at once I ship roughly twice as much before end of day.
testimonials.author: Indie developer

testimonials.quote: One git worktree per workspace means agents never step on each other — parallel refactors no longer risk breaking someone else's branch.
testimonials.author: Backend engineer

testimonials.quote: The self-hosted team edition runs on our own machines, so code never leaves the network. Our compliance team finally signed off.
testimonials.author: Engineering lead

bottom_cta.headline: Ready to get started?
bottom_cta.subhead: Download the desktop edition, or explore Self-Hosted Team.

changelog.subtitle: Release notes for every desktop app version, newest first.
changelog.netdisk_desc: Mainland China users can download the installers via Baidu Netdisk (recommended when GitHub is slow).
